question.text: Under which section of the Companies Act, 2013 can the Registrar of Companies strike off a defunct company?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 248 of the Companies Act, 2013 confers powers on the Registrar to strike a defunct company off the register, after completing the formalities prescribed in the section. **The Trap:** Section 206 is about calling for information, Section 209 about seizure of documents, and Section 81 about maintaining registers of charges. Each has distinct powers. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** ROC has progressive powers: Section 206 (call for info) → Section 209 (seize documents) → Section 248 (strike off). Know the hierarchy for compliance purposes.
